column1: 5-YEAR FINANCIAL MODEL — Design-Led Desk Gear Brand
Prepared 2026-06-26. Currency USD unless noted. FX assumption 1 USD = 1.42 CAD (verify - see Report 03 notes).
All figures modeled from sourced unit economics (Report 03_sourcing). Volume ramp is an ASSUMPTION; widen uncertainty in later years.
=== BLOCK A: UNIT ECONOMICS (per unit USD) ===

column5: Hero single sells ~$29; blended ASP used below is $42 (singles+kits+promo)
At $49. At blended $42 ASP ~ $20.10/unit (used in P&L)

column1: STRESS TEST (price -10% to $44.10 AND cost +10% to $12.64)

column5: Both channels stay POSITIVE. DTC 19.2% / Walmart 38.5%

column1: === BLOCK B: YEAR 1 MONTHLY P&L + CASH FLOW — BASE CASE ===
Assumptions: blended ASP $42; contribution-before-marketing $20.10/unit; variable selling $10.40/unit; start cash $40000; founder takes no salary Y1.
Phase 0 (M1-2 CA validation) | Phase 1 US entry M3-4 | Q4 ramp M10-11 | Jan reset M12

column4: Net margin ~36% (no founder salary; outsourced ops)
Higher %: weak organic forces paid spend
Downside ~ cash-flow breakeven IF you stop spending

column3: Do NOT reinvest; hold or exit

(Later-year figures carry WIDENING uncertainty; treat as directional toward the Grovemade-scale goal)

column2: Product fails to gain traction; trigger exit/pivot by mid-Y2

column3: Survives base & conservative; $30k survives base only, thin
Then fund next SKU from cashflow

column2: Funds first production run before manufacturing; demand validated with real money
